critical.mandate: NEVER write to any memory file without explicit user approval at the confirmation gate (Step 3).
This workflow requires model_override: opus. If running on a weaker model, HALT with a clear error.
Val communicates diplomatically — present findings as observations, not accusations. Use neutral phrasing.
Decision-log entries MUST use the standardized header format: ### [YYYY-MM-DD] Decision Title with Agent/Workflow/Sprint/Status/Related metadata block.
Conversation-context uses REPLACE semantics — overwrite body content, preserve file header.
Ground-truth uses APPEND/UPDATE semantics — new facts appended, contradicted facts annotated with "Superseded [YYYY-MM-DD]: {reason}".
Load memory-management skill sections JIT per step — release between steps.

step.title: Load Session Context
step.action: Accept session findings as input context. Sources: — Upstream workflow invocation (e.g., val-validate-artifact passing findings as context) — User-provided context directly (standalone invocation via /gaia-val-save)
step.check: Report "No session findings to save — nothing to persist." End workflow gracefully. Do NOT proceed to Step 2.
step.action: Load memory-management:session-load skill section JIT.

Read current state of all three memory files in _memory/validator-sidecar/ (if they exist): — decision-log.md: note last entry date and count — conversation-context.md: note current session summary — ground-truth.md: note section structure and approximate size If any file is missing, note it for initialization in Step 4.
Release memory-management:session-load skill section.

step.title: Summarize Session
step.action: Load memory-management:decision-formatting and memory-management:context-summarization skill sections JIT.

Produce three distinct summaries from the session findings:
**(a) Decision-Log Entries** — Format each decision/finding using the standardized header: ```markdown ### [YYYY-MM-DD] Decision Title
- **Agent:** validator - **Workflow:** {originating workflow name, e.g., val-save-session or val-validate-artifact} - **Sprint:** {sprint_id or "N/A"} - **Status:** recorded - **Related:** {FR-IDs, ADR-IDs, story keys as applicable}
{Domain-specific body — findings, rationale, context} ```
**(b) Conversation-Context Snapshot** — Summarize what was validated in this session: - What artifact(s) or area(s) were examined - Key findings produced (counts and severity) - Decisions made (approved, deferred, rejected) - Current state of work (what the user is focused on) This replaces the previous conversation-context content entirely.
**(c) Ground-Truth Updates** — List changes to verified facts: - New facts to add (with section placement) - Existing facts that were contradicted (old value, new value, reason for change) - Contradicted facts get "Superseded [YYYY-MM-DD]: {reason}" annotation on old entry If no ground-truth changes were discovered, state "No ground-truth updates for this session."
Release memory-management:decision-formatting and memory-management:context-summarization skill sections.

step.title: User Confirmation Gate
step.action: Present all three summaries in a structured, readable format with clear section headers.
step.template-output: Session save preview (ephemeral — deleted after workflow completes) containing: — Decision-log entries to append (count and formatted content) — Conversation-context snapshot to replace (full content) — Ground-truth updates to apply (new facts and contradictions)
step.ask:
Review the session save preview above.
**[a] Approve and save all** — write all three summaries to memory files **[e] Edit** — modify findings before saving (re-presents for approval after edits) **[d] Discard** — cancel save entirely, no changes to memory
Choose [a], [e], or [d]:
step.action: Report "Session findings discarded — no changes to memory." End workflow cleanly. Do NOT proceed to Step 4 or 5.
Present summaries in editable form. Allow user to modify, remove, or add entries. After edits, re-present updated summaries and return to the [a]/[e]/[d] prompt for final confirmation.
Proceed to Step 4.

step.title: Write to Memory Files
step.action: Load memory-management:session-save skill section JIT.
Check if _memory/validator-sidecar/ directory exists. If not, create it.

Check if each of the three memory files exists. For any missing file, create it with its header template:
**decision-log.md template:** ```markdown # Val Validator — Decision Log
> Chronological record of validation decisions, findings, and session outcomes. > Format: Standardized header per architecture Memory Format Standardization spec.
--- ```
**conversation-context.md template:** ```markdown # Val Validator — Conversation Context
> Rolling summary of the most recent validation session. > This file is replaced (not appended) on each session save.
---
No sessions recorded yet. ```
**ground-truth.md template:** ```markdown # Val Validator — Ground Truth
> Verified facts about the framework and project. Cumulative and auditable. > New facts are appended. Contradicted facts are annotated with "Superseded" notes. > Budget: 200K tokens. Warn at 80% (160K tokens). Compress via /gaia-val-refresh-ground-truth.
--- ```
**Write (a) — Decision Log:** Append the formatted decision-log entries to the end of _memory/validator-sidecar/decision-log.md. Each entry uses the standardized header format.

**Write (b) — Conversation Context:** Replace the body content of _memory/validator-sidecar/conversation-context.md with the fresh session snapshot. Preserve the file header (everything above and including the first `---` separator). Replace everything after it.

**Write (c) — Ground Truth:** Before writing, estimate the post-save token count using 4 chars/token approximation: — Read current file size — Add estimated size of new content — If post-save exceeds 80% of 200K tokens (160K tokens): display warning "Ground truth will be at {current}K/{max}K tokens after this save. Consider running /gaia-val-refresh-ground-truth to compress." — The warning is informational only — proceed with the save. Append new facts under appropriate sections. For contradicted facts: annotate the old entry with "Superseded [YYYY-MM-DD]: {reason}" and append the corrected fact.
Release memory-management:session-save skill section.

step.title: Post-Save Verification
step.action: Read back each modified file and verify writes succeeded: — decision-log.md: confirm new entry/entries appear at the end of the file — conversation-context.md: confirm fresh session snapshot is present (not old content) — ground-truth.md: confirm new facts and/or superseded annotations are present

Report verification results: — If all writes confirmed: "{N} decision(s) logged, conversation context updated, {M} ground truth fact(s) added/updated." — If any write failed: warn specifically which file failed and what was expected vs. found. Do NOT report success if a write failed.
